parsed_review.summary: CP00-321 is a descriptor-only RP10 (Intake Conflict Engagement) bootstrap pack reviewed in its pre-final-closeout state. The pack-level posture is honest and well-supported: command-evidence and manifest both declare production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review and gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending, so there is no over-claim of production readiness. The authority boundary is respected end-to-end — registry/service/contract encode claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, and the manifest restates claude_not_final_approver=true and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are thoroughly enforced: the intake source contains no writeFile/fetch/child_process/db calls (only descriptor booleans set false), no_write_attestation freezes all runtime/write/storage/Hermes-receipt flags to false, and fixtures carry no real data. Coverage is internally consistent and matches the baseline (P00=52 + P01=98 = 150 units across 20 descriptor sections; deliverable distribution implementation:104/contract:3/security_audit:6/ui:19/fixture:3/test:9/hermes_evidence:3/claude_review:3 and the micro-title distribution both reconcile to 150). Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic evidence descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. The readiness auth-preflight passed with a non-empty valid-JSON result, and this review was conducted read-only via Read/Grep/Glob. The only defects found are three CP00-299 copy-paste artifacts that do not affect gates, security, or boundaries: two wrong-pack manifest references on inert, fail-safe fallback paths in the validation harnesses (P2), and one dangling README documentation anchor mirrored self-consistently into the contract (P3). No P0/P1 issues; the pack does not weaken any production_ready gate. Final enterprise-trust approval remains with the human approver — this review is advisory input only.

findings.title: Validator fallback binds to wrong pack manifest (cp00-299)
findings.details: Line 33 reads `../docs/closeout-packs/cp00-299/manifest.json` into a variable named `cp321Manifest`, and line 34 uses it as the fallback plan source: `closeoutPlan.packs.find(p => p.pack_id === 'CP00-321') ?? cp321Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot`. The path targets a different, real pack (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-299/manifest.json exists). Today the primary lookup resolves because CP00-321 is present in closeout-pack-plan.json (line 409), so the fallback is inert and the rp10:intake-core:validate gate passes. If the plan lookup ever returned undefined, the harness would bind CP00-321 validation to CP00-299's plan_binding_snapshot; validateIntakeCoreCp321Coverage would then fail on the pack_id==='CP00-321' assertion (fail-safe — it cannot yield a false PASS). This is a copy-paste artifact from the CP00-299 template and a maintainability/trust hazard in evidence-producing code.
findings.recommendation: Retarget the fallback path to docs/closeout-packs/cp00-321/manifest.json and rename the variable to reflect cp00-321.

findings.title: Unit test fallback references wrong pack manifest (cp00-299)
findings.details: Line 26 sets `cp321ManifestPath` to `../../../docs/closeout-packs/cp00-299/manifest.json` and line 28 uses `cp321Manifest?.plan_binding_snapshot` as the fallback when CP00-321 is absent from the plan — the same copy-paste artifact as the validator (F1). Because the cp00-299 manifest exists, the wrong snapshot would load on the fallback path, but the primary plan lookup currently resolves CP00-321 so the fallback is inert and `npm test` passes. Behavior is fail-safe (a CP00-299 snapshot fails the CP00-321 pack_id/range assertions), so it cannot mask a defect, but the misleading reference should be corrected.
findings.recommendation: Retarget the manifest path and variable name to cp00-321 to match the pack under test.

findings.title: Dangling documentation_entry anchor #cp00-299
findings.details: The descriptor sets `documentation_entry: 'packages/intake/README.md#cp00-299'`, but packages/intake/README.md only contains a `## CP00-321` section (anchor #cp00-321) with no cp00-299 content, so the anchor is dangling. The identical stale value is mirrored in contracts/intake-core-contract.json:2252, which keeps the contract-vs-descriptor deepEqual in the validator passing (self-consistent). Purely a documentation pointer drift with no gate, security, coverage, or boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Update the anchor to packages/intake/README.md#cp00-321 in service.js and regenerate the contract so the mirrored field matches.
